svg.title: Cassette emergency stop and interlock wiring
svg.desc: 24 V DC dry-contact emergency stop hardwired loop between platform E-stop input and output at ELEC ECP, with Novec panel, VESDA, IMD, Jetson BMS, and abort stations wired into the chain. Main disconnect trip coil receives hardwired signal — fail-safe without BMS. All signals also mirrored to BMS for telemetry.
svg.text: Cassette E-stop and safety interlocks — hardwired loop
24 V DC dry contact. Fail-safe (normally closed). Independent of BMS for trip authority.
Trip authority — hardwired to main disconnect coil

g.text: 800 V DC main disconnect trip coil
Any NC contact opens → trip within 100 ms
Independent of BMS, Jetson, or network
Racks go down — GPU caps handle 100 ms transient

svg.text: Blue dashed = BMS log/mirror path (not trip authority)
Red solid = hardwired trip chain (fail-safe, works without BMS)
Interlock actions on E-stop assertion (BMS-executed, ≤ 2 s)

g.text: T = 0 to T = 32 s · 30-second hold window
Either ECP abort key-switch cancels Novec discharge
Abort does NOT restore rack power — requires manual authorization at ELEC ECP
After T = 32 s, abort is ineffective — agent released per FIRE-001 §9

svg.text: Trip response time ≤ 100 ms (main disconnect) · Detection-to-trip ≤ 2 s per ELEC-001 §2 / FIRE-001 §9
Platform E-stop OUT completes the loop — pod-originated faults propagate upstream to platform BESS
